description: Supabase is an open-source backend-as-a-service platform and Firebase alternative. It provides a PostgreSQL database, authentication, file storage, instant APIs, edge functions, and realtime capabilities in a unified platform.

limitations: Projects pause after 1 week of inactivity

features.spend_cap: Enabled by default to prevent runaway bills

features.same_quota_and_overage: Same 100 GB quota and overage pricing as Pro

features.custom_quotas: Custom quotas and private cloud deployment

paid_add_ons.name: Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR)
paid_add_ons.notes: Enterprise plan supports up to 28-day retention

key_features.description: Full PostgreSQL database with extensions support, table-oriented relational data model

Prebuilt auth/user management system with social providers: Google, Facebook, Apple, Azure (Microsoft), Twitter, GitHub, GitLab, BitBucket, Discord, Keycloak, LinkedIn, Notion, Slack, Spotify, Twitch, WorkOS, and Zoom
Subscribe to Postgres changes in real-time, broadcast messages to multiple clients, track user presence and online status
Store and serve large files with storage buckets (S3-compatible object storage), eliminating need for separate S3 integration
Serverless Deno-based functions running close to users for reduced latency. Best suited for lightweight serverless logic.
Auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs from your database schema
Supabase CLI for local development and project management
Web dashboard with Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoints for health insights

notable_details: Positioned as a Firebase alternative with SQL (PostgreSQL) instead of NoSQL
Spend cap enabled by default on Pro plan to prevent unexpected bills
Free tier is one of the most generous in the BaaS space
Typically 30-50% cheaper than Firebase at scale due to resource-based pricing vs per-operation pricing
More predictable pricing than AWS — equivalent AWS setup estimated at $80-120/month vs $25 Pro plan
Projects pause after 1 week of inactivity on Free plan
Realistic production Pro plan baseline is ~$110-120/month when including dedicated Large compute
